title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.

Gaza's children, traumatized and mutilated by war: 'His prosthesis hurts, but he endures it to show that he isn't different'
subhead: Due to the bombings carried out by Israel since October 7, 2023, the Gaza Strip now holds the grim record for the highest number of amputee children per capita in the world, according to the World Health Organization. These children have been trying to rebuild their lives despite forced displacement and lack of medical care in a devastated territory.

title: 'Netanyahu still regards the 1946 King David Hotel bombers in Jerusalem as heroes'
subhead: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has never stopped defending the attack, which claimed the highest-ever number of civilian lives in British Mandate Palestine, historian Jean-Pierre Filiu writes in his column.

title: 'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
subhead: The excessive commercialization and blatant conflicts of interest that have characterized the 2026 World Cup should convince governments, athletes and other sports stakeholders to take back control, argue researchers Frédérique Reynertz and Pim Verschuuren.

title: Is the Bible terrifying? Three frightening stories from the holy book
subhead: The biblical corpus shared by Judaism and Christianity contains scenes of extraordinary violence, sometimes embodied by terrifying figures. For centuries, such passages have puzzled scholars and fascinated readers.

19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
subhead: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Elie Barnavi: 'Marwan Barghouti is the only one capable of uniting the Palestinian people around a national project and the two-state solution'
subhead: The former Israeli ambassador to France, in an op-ed for Le Monde, calls for the release of the Palestinian politician, who has been held in Israel since 2002. He argues that Barghouti has consistently championed the project of a sovereign Palestine living in peace alongside Israel.

title: 'It still feels a bit unreal': Lovers of Fontainebleau forest 'deeply saddened' by devastating fire
subhead: Naturalists, hikers, and other lovers of the most famous woods in the Paris region are the loss of rich ecosystems and wonder about the future of an area that has long been popular.
